title: Verify That a Robot Resumes Its Transaction After Restart
summary: Use this source-based verification procedure to confirm the documented distinction between robot restart and robot removal. The source states that if a robot is in the middle of a transaction, it resumes that transaction after restart, and that task loss is associated with removing the robot from the system, after which it must go through initialization.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing the outcome of a robot restart and determining whether post-restart behavior matches the training source statement about transaction persistence.
not_for: Do not use this as a robot restart execution procedure; the source candidate is for verification of behavior, not for performing the restart.
Do not use this to treat robot restart and robot removal as equivalent conditions.
Do not use this as a detailed removal recovery procedure; the source only states that removal leads to initialization and task loss.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review robot status and restart outcome against the documented source distinction between restart and removal.

safety_notes: Do not assume removal and restart have the same operational outcome; the source explicitly distinguishes them.
If the robot was removed from the system, the source indicates it must go through initialization and that this is the case where the task is considered lost.
access_or_tools_needed: Knowledge of whether the robot was restarted or removed from the system

access_required: Access to view robot transaction status
Access to determine whether the robot was restarted or removed from the system
system_preconditions: A robot restart outcome is available for review
The robot was in the middle of a transaction before the observed event, if transaction-resume behavior is being verified

external_references.description: Training segment stating that in-progress transactions resume after restart and are only lost when the robot is removed from the system.

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript segment covering robot restart behavior and stating that tasks usually resume after restart unless the robot is removed from the system.

steps.title: Identify the restarted robot and transaction state
steps.purpose: Establish that the observed case is a restart review for a robot that was in the middle of a transaction.
steps.instruction: Identify a robot that was restarted while in the middle of a transaction, and confirm that the case under review is a restart outcome rather than another type of robot state change.
steps.expected_result: A specific robot and its pre-restart in-transaction condition are identified for verification.
steps.healthy_condition: The reviewed case clearly involves a restarted robot that was in the middle of a transaction.
steps.failure_condition: The robot state before the event is unclear, or it is not clear whether the event was a restart or some other action such as removal.

actions.label: Identify restarted robot
actions.exact_action: Identify a robot that was restarted while in the middle of a transaction.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot restart context and transcript note that tasks usually resume after restart unless the robot is removed from the system.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if you cannot determine whether the robot was restarted or removed from the system.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'if it's in the middle of the transaction, we'll resume the transaction after the restart'.

steps.title: Check whether the transaction resumes after restart
steps.purpose: Verify the primary documented expectation for restart behavior.
steps.instruction: After the restart completes, check whether the robot resumes the transaction, as stated in the source.
steps.expected_result: The robot resumes the in-progress transaction after restart.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed post-restart behavior matches the source statement that the transaction resumes after restart.
steps.failure_condition: The restarted robot does not resume the transaction.

actions.label: Verify resumed transaction
actions.exact_action: After the restart completes, check whether the robot resumes the transaction, as stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript statement that if the robot is in the middle of the transaction, it will resume the transaction after restart.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a restarted robot does not resume its transaction and there is no evidence that it was removed from the system.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'if it's in the middle of the transaction, we'll resume the transaction after the restart'.
The segment explicitly states that transactions resume after restart and are only lost when the robot is removed from the system.

steps.title: Distinguish restart behavior from removal behavior
steps.purpose: Prevent misclassification of the observed outcome by applying the source's explicit distinction.
steps.instruction: If reviewing the restart outcome, distinguish restart behavior from removal behavior using the source statement that tasks do not get lost for restarts.
steps.expected_result: The case is classified using the source distinction that restart preserves the task while removal is the loss case.
steps.healthy_condition: Restart is treated as task-preserving behavior in line with the source.
steps.failure_condition: Restart and removal are treated as equivalent, or task loss is incorrectly attributed to restart without evidence of removal.

actions.label: Apply restart versus removal distinction
actions.exact_action: Distinguish restart behavior from removal behavior using the source statement that tasks do not get lost for restarts.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript note that the task does not get lost for restarts.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if available evidence suggests the robot may have been removed rather than restarted.
Do not treat restart and removal as equivalent; the source explicitly distinguishes them.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'the task doesn't get lost for restarts... The only time... the task is... lost is if you remove it... then it has to go through initialization process.'

steps.title: Handle the case where the robot was removed from the system
steps.purpose: Apply the source's documented exception case for task loss.
steps.instruction: If the robot was removed from the system rather than restarted, note that the source says it must go through an initialization process and that this is when the task is considered lost.
steps.expected_result: The case is identified as a removal case, with initialization required and task loss understood as source-supported behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: Removal cases are recognized as distinct from restart cases and are documented accordingly.
steps.failure_condition: A removal case is incorrectly evaluated using restart expectations.

actions.label: Identify removal exception
actions.exact_action: If the robot was removed from the system rather than restarted, note that the source says it must go through an initialization process and that this is when the task is considered lost.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection add/remove workflow showing removal as a distinct system action.
Transcript statement that task loss occurs when the robot is removed and must go through initialization.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the robot was removed from the system and the review is still being evaluated as a restart persistence case.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'The only time... the task is... lost is if you remove it... then it has to go through initialization process.'
Robot Selection add/remove workflow shows removal as a distinct action from restart.

steps.title: Record whether observed behavior matches the documented distinction
steps.purpose: Capture the verification result against the source-supported expectation.
steps.instruction: Record whether the observed behavior matches the documented distinction between restart and removal.
steps.expected_result: A clear verification result is documented: either restart matched expected transaction resumption, or removal matched the documented initialization/task-loss case.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded outcome aligns with the source-supported distinction and clearly states which condition was observed.
steps.failure_condition: The outcome is undocumented, ambiguous, or inconsistent with the source distinction.

actions.label: Document verification result
actions.exact_action: Record whether the observed behavior matches the documented distinction between restart and removal.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Source segment summarizing the expected distinction to use when recording the result.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if observed behavior does not match the documented distinction and there is no evidence supporting removal from the system.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The segment explicitly states that transactions resume after restart and are only lost when the robot is removed from the system.

success_criteria: The reviewer confirms whether a restarted robot resumed its in-progress transaction.
The reviewer correctly distinguishes restart behavior from removal behavior using the source statement.
The final record states whether the observed outcome matches the documented source expectation.
healthy_conditions: A robot that was in the middle of a transaction resumes that transaction after restart.
Restart cases are treated as not losing the task.
Removal cases are recognized as requiring initialization and as the case where the task is considered lost.
failure_conditions: A restarted robot does not resume its transaction and there is no evidence that it was removed from the system.
Restart and removal are treated as equivalent despite the source distinction.
The observed case cannot be clearly classified as restart versus removal.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if a restarted robot does not resume its transaction and there is no evidence that it was removed from the system.
Escalate if the available evidence is insufficient to determine whether the robot was restarted or removed.
Do not classify a removal case as a restart persistence failure.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source segment for restart behavior, transaction resumption, and removal exception.
Robot Selection add/remove workflow showing removal as a distinct action.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Transcript: 'if it's in the middle of the transaction, we'll resume the transaction after the restart' and 'the task doesn't get lost for restarts... The only time... the task is... lost is if you remove it... then it has to go through initialization process.'
The segment explicitly states that transactions resume after restart and are only lost when the robot is removed from the system.

missing_details: The source does not provide a detailed UI path or exact screen for checking transaction status.
The source does not provide a formal logging location or documentation template for recording the verification result.
The source does not provide a time expectation for restart completion or verification duration.
The source does not provide a detailed removal recovery workflow beyond noting initialization and task loss.
